title: Income & Asset Module Bank (Tasks 1–18)
description: Choose-your-own module bank for a mobile-first AI income programme. Each module is a monetisable, locally-owned digital asset that participants learn to build and sell.

field_glossary.title: Module name.
field_glossary.role_label: The practitioner role a participant adopts for this module.
field_glossary.asset: The deliverable (‘The Asset’) produced and sold.
field_glossary.execution: How participants build the asset (‘The Execution’).
field_glossary.energy_maximisation: Battery / bandwidth strategy for low-resource settings.
field_glossary.why_it_monetises: The market demand that makes the asset sellable.
field_glossary.ai_basics_learned: AI / digital skills taught, as a tag list.
field_glossary.ethical_rule: An ethical constraint, where the source specifies one (else null).
field_glossary.safety_rule: A safety constraint, where the source specifies one (else null).
field_glossary.languages: Languages explicitly named for this module (else empty).
field_glossary.citations: Supporting references cited in the source for this module.
field_glossary.notes: Extra source instructions attached to this module.
field_glossary.expanded_variant_of: If this module is an expanded re-statement of an earlier one, its id (else null).
field_glossary.source_marked_with_asterisk: True if the source heading carried an asterisk (modules 15 and 18).

modules.title: AI Security Red-Teaming & Bias Benchmarking
modules.asset: A regional testing framework that evaluates whether AI systems produce biased, unsafe, culturally inaccurate, or linguistically weak responses.
modules.execution: Participants create test prompts in French, Brazilian Portuguese, Spanish, English, and Wolof. They ask AI systems to respond to local names, places, proverbs, government-service questions, migration questions, education questions, health-adjacent questions, and market scenarios. They log failures in a spreadsheet: prompt, AI answer, problem type, corrected answer, severity level.
modules.energy_maximisation: Prompts can be written offline in batches. Testing is done only when connectivity is available. Results are stored as small text files or CSVs.
modules.why_it_monetises: AI companies, NGOs, universities, and firms deploying chatbots need localised testing before they release products in new regions. Participants become external cultural and linguistic QA testers.
modules.community_multiplier: The community gains safer local AI tools that do not erase its languages, names, customs, or realities.

modules.title: Decoupled Web-Scraping & Local Market Aggregation
modules.asset: Clean local datasets on commodity prices, rents, transport routes, school fees, water delivery, phone repair, clinic hours, or informal market prices.
modules.execution: Using lightweight mobile tools such as a terminal emulator, spreadsheet app, or browser text mode, participants collect public data and manually verify it in the community. They produce a weekly CSV and a one-page market intelligence brief.
modules.energy_maximisation: Text-only collection uses far less battery than video or graphic-heavy browsing. Participants run scripts or enter data offline, then sync CSVs when connected.
modules.why_it_monetises: Local logistics firms, traders, farmers, researchers, diaspora investors, and NGOs often need fresh local data but lack reliable field-level information.
modules.community_multiplier: Small sellers gain better pricing power because they can compare markets instead of relying only on middlemen.
modules.ethical_rule: Collect public or consent-based data only. Do not scrape private accounts, personal messages, or restricted websites.

modules.title: Multilingual Data Annotation & Local Language Validation
modules.asset: Validated text, speech, and translation datasets in French, Portuguese, Spanish, English, and Wolof.
modules.execution: Participants label short sentences by topic, intent, emotion, politeness level, urgency, dialect, or customer-service category. They also record and transcribe short consent-based voice samples. For Wolof and other underrepresented languages, human verification is essential because African languages remain a weak point in many AI systems.
modules.energy_maximisation: Audio is recorded offline. Files are compressed before upload. Labeling is done in short battery-saving sessions.
modules.why_it_monetises: AI labs, call centers, universities, speech-recognition firms, translation companies, and NGOs need local language data to improve models.
modules.community_multiplier: Participants help make their languages visible in the AI economy rather than excluded from it.

citations.label: UNESCO Courier
citations.title: African languages: a blind spot in AI

modules.title: AI-Assisted Microbusiness Content Studio
modules.asset: Low-cost marketing packs for street vendors, tailors, hairdressers, mechanics, tutors, food sellers, farmers, and artisans.
modules.execution: Participants interview a business owner by voice note, then use AI to create: business description, WhatsApp status copy, Facebook post, SMS promotion, product list, simple flyer text, customer reply templates, and a mini FAQ.
modules.energy_maximisation: Most work is text-based. Images are optional and should be low-resolution unless the client specifically pays for design.
modules.why_it_monetises: Many microbusinesses need digital visibility but cannot hire a full agency. Participants can sell small weekly or monthly content bundles.
modules.community_multiplier: Local businesses gain more customers, and participants become neighbourhood digital-service providers.

modules.title: Mobile CV, Scholarship, and Job Application Clinic
modules.asset: AI-assisted CVs, résumés, cover letters, scholarship essays, apprenticeship applications, LinkedIn-style summaries, and interview preparation sheets.
modules.execution: Participants collect client information through a simple intake form or voice note. They produce documents in English, French, Spanish, or Portuguese, and where useful, oral explanation in Wolof.
modules.energy_maximisation: Templates are stored offline. AI is used only for drafting and improving. Final editing can happen without internet.
modules.why_it_monetises: Job seekers, students, migrants, apprentices, and informal workers often need polished documents but lack access to professional writing support.
modules.community_multiplier: Better applications can help more people access jobs, grants, training programmes, and scholarships.

modules.title: Voice-Note Transcription, Translation & Meeting Summary Service
modules.asset: Clean meeting minutes, translated voice notes, action lists, attendance summaries, and decision logs.
modules.execution: Participants receive consent-based audio from community meetings, cooperatives, parent groups, small businesses, religious groups, or training sessions. They transcribe, summarise, translate, and format the results.
modules.energy_maximisation: Audio is recorded offline. Transcription can be done in segments. Syncing happens after compression or over shared Wi-Fi.
modules.why_it_monetises: Many local organisations need records but do not have administrative staff. This service converts informal spoken knowledge into usable documents.
modules.community_multiplier: Groups become better organised because decisions, tasks, and responsibilities are preserved.
modules.ethical_rule: Never record or process meetings without consent. Do not handle sensitive legal, medical, or political material unless the programme has strict safeguards.

modules.title: Community Knowledge Base & FAQ Bot Builder
modules.asset: Small community knowledge bases: “How to register for school,” “Where to charge a phone,” “How to find clinic hours,” “How to apply for training,” “Where to sell produce,” or “How to report a service problem.”
modules.execution: Participants gather verified local information, convert it into question-and-answer format, translate it into programme languages, and create a simple chatbot script or WhatsApp response guide. Progressive web apps and offline-first methods are relevant because PWAs can be installable and designed to work more reliably across devices.
modules.energy_maximisation: The knowledge base is stored as plain text, CSV, or lightweight HTML. Updates are batched weekly.
modules.why_it_monetises: Schools, NGOs, clinics, local councils, churches, mosques, cooperatives, and small businesses need reliable information systems but cannot always afford custom software.
modules.community_multiplier: Residents save time and money because essential information becomes easier to access.

modules.title: AI-Assisted Agricultural, Fishing, or Market Advisory Digest
modules.asset: Short, verified, local-language digests for farmers, fishers, food sellers, transporters, and market women/men.
modules.execution: Participants collect local prices, buyer needs, weather advisories from trusted sources, pest alerts from agricultural extension officers, and community observations. AI helps convert the information into a short bulletin: “What changed, what it means, what to do next.”
modules.energy_maximisation: The digest is text-first. Voice versions are short. Distribution can happen by SMS, WhatsApp broadcast, printed notice, or community radio script.
modules.why_it_monetises: Subscribers may pay a small weekly fee. Cooperatives, NGOs, and traders may sponsor the digest.
modules.community_multiplier: Better market information can reduce exploitation and improve timing for selling or transporting goods.
modules.safety_rule: Do not present AI output as expert agricultural, legal, medical, or financial advice. Label it as a verified information digest and cite local sources.

modules.title: AI Bookkeeping, Inventory & Receipt Digitisation for Microvendors
modules.asset: Simple digital ledgers, inventory sheets, receipt summaries, expense categories, profit estimates, and reorder alerts.
modules.execution: Participants photograph or manually enter sales and expenses, then use AI to categorise entries and produce a weekly business summary. The final product can be a spreadsheet, PDF, or handwritten-friendly report.
modules.energy_maximisation: Photos are taken only when needed. Text entry is preferred. Calculations happen offline in spreadsheets.
modules.why_it_monetises: Vendors often lose money because they cannot see profit, stock movement, or debt clearly. Participants can charge for weekly bookkeeping support.
modules.community_multiplier: Microbusinesses become more resilient and may qualify more easily for loans, grants, cooperative purchasing, or supplier credit.

modules.title: Local Map, Directory & Digital Presence Repair
modules.asset: Verified business listings, opening hours, service descriptions, product photos, location notes, and customer-response templates.
modules.execution: Participants help local businesses create or correct their digital profiles, produce short descriptions in multiple languages, and create a small “find us” card that can be shared by WhatsApp or printed.
modules.energy_maximisation: One field visit can collect data for several businesses. Photos are compressed. Text profiles are drafted offline.
modules.why_it_monetises: Businesses lose customers when their hours, phone numbers, or locations are wrong. Participants can charge a setup fee plus a small update fee.
modules.community_multiplier: More accurate local directories help customers, delivery workers, tourists, NGOs, and emergency responders.
modules.ethical_rule: Only update listings with owner permission.
notes: Field-safety note: travel in groups and never alone, no matter the gender.

modules.title: Community Research, Survey & Impact Reporting Service
modules.asset: Consent-based survey data, interview summaries, community needs assessments, programme feedback reports, and simple dashboards.
modules.execution: Participants design short surveys, collect responses offline, translate answers, code themes, and produce a report for NGOs, schools, clinics, cooperatives, or local leaders.
modules.energy_maximisation: Forms are completed offline. Data syncs in batches. Reports are text-first with small charts only when necessary.
modules.why_it_monetises: Organisations need evidence for grants, programme improvement, donor reporting, and community planning. Participants can sell research support without needing a full computer lab.
modules.community_multiplier: Communities become producers of their own evidence rather than subjects of outside research.
modules.ethical_rule: Use informed consent. Avoid collecting unnecessary personal data. Protect minors and vulnerable participants.

modules.title: Hyper-Local Data Corpora
modules.asset: Text datasets, structured dictionaries, and colloquial audio files in regional languages such as Haitian Creole, Wolof, Bambara, and other underrepresented local languages, formatted into JSON pairs or other machine-readable structures for AI model training and fine-tuning.
modules.execution: Participants collect local words, idioms, proverbs, pronunciation samples, short dialogues, marketplace phrases, school-related phrases, public-service terms, and everyday expressions. They translate or align them across French, Brazilian Portuguese, Spanish, English, and Wolof where appropriate. The final product may include audio clips, transcriptions, translations, definitions, sample sentences, and metadata.
modules.energy_maximisation: Most collection can happen offline through notebooks, voice recordings, and simple text files. Participants batch-record audio while phones are charged, compress files, and upload only when they reach Wi-Fi, a solar kiosk, or another power/connectivity point.
modules.why_it_monetises: Large technology companies, universities, translation firms, NGOs, and AI developers need high-quality local language data because many AI systems struggle with regional dialects, code-switching, local context, and culturally specific meanings.
modules.community_multiplier: Local languages become economically valuable digital assets rather than being treated as obstacles to technology access. Communities gain stronger representation in future AI systems.
modules.safety_rule: Collect language and voice data only with consent. Do not record private conversations, children, sacred/community-restricted knowledge, or politically sensitive material without proper safeguards.

modules.title: API-Driven FinTech Micro-SaaS
modules.asset: Lightweight, mobile-first software tools that connect with regional payment systems or mobile wallets, such as MonCash, Wave, Orange Money, or similar local platforms.
modules.execution: Participants design small tools for informal merchants, cooperatives, tutors, delivery riders, market sellers, or savings groups. Examples include automated receipt generation, simple inventory logs, layaway tracking, informal escrow records, payment reminders, and transaction summaries.
modules.energy_maximisation: The tools should be text-light, form-based, and offline-first whenever possible. Data can be stored locally on the phone and synced later. Participants should avoid building heavy graphics, continuous background processes, or always-online dashboards.
modules.why_it_monetises: Informal businesses already use mobile money but often lack receipts, records, accounting, trust mechanisms, and inventory systems. Participants can charge setup fees, monthly maintenance fees, or small transaction-based service fees.
modules.community_multiplier: Microbusinesses gain better records, more customer trust, and stronger financial visibility, which can help with loans, cooperative purchasing, savings groups, and grant applications.
modules.safety_rule: Do not store sensitive financial data insecurely. Do not ask for wallet PINs, passwords, private keys, or verification codes. Participants should build tools that record transactions, not tools that control someone else’s money without formal legal safeguards.

modules.title: Geocentric & Algorithmic E-Commerce Bundles
modules.asset: Digital storefronts, print-on-demand designs, cultural graphics, templates, social media packs, and product bundles based on local aesthetics, patterns, proverbs, histories, landscapes, and artistic traditions.
modules.execution: Participants use AI to create product concepts, captions, store descriptions, mockups, and multilingual listings. They may sell digital art, printable posters, T-shirt designs, educational flashcards, cultural quote cards, tourist guides, language-learning cards, or community-themed design bundles.
modules.energy_maximisation: Design ideation and text drafting can happen offline. Image generation or upload-heavy work should be batched during charging and Wi-Fi periods. Participants should save reusable templates to avoid regenerating large files repeatedly.
modules.why_it_monetises: This turns local cultural knowledge and design sense into exportable digital products for diaspora communities, tourists, language learners, educators, and global buyers interested in authentic regional aesthetics.
modules.community_multiplier: Local culture becomes a source of income rather than something extracted by outsiders. Participants can collaborate with local artists, elders, storytellers, and craftspeople.
modules.safety_rule: Do not exploit sacred symbols, restricted cultural knowledge, or another artist’s work without permission. Use AI as an assistant, not as a tool for cultural theft.

modules.title: AI Security Red-Teaming & Bias Benchmarking
modules.asset: Testing frameworks that evaluate how AI systems respond to regional inputs, local languages, community names, cultural references, security-sensitive prompts, and identity-related scenarios.
modules.execution: Participants create test prompts in French, Brazilian Portuguese, Spanish, English, Wolof, and other relevant languages. They document whether the AI system hallucinates, stereotypes, mistranslates, gives unsafe advice, misunderstands local realities, or fails to serve the community accurately.
modules.energy_maximisation: Prompts and rubrics can be written offline. Testing can happen in short online sessions. Results are saved in CSV or spreadsheet format and uploaded in batches.
modules.why_it_monetises: Companies, NGOs, universities, and government-adjacent organisations need to audit AI tools before deploying them in multilingual or culturally complex settings. Participants can sell themselves as regional AI testers and bias-benchmarking contractors.
modules.community_multiplier: Communities gain safer and more accurate AI systems because local people help identify failures before those systems are widely deployed.
modules.safety_rule: Red-teaming should expose weaknesses responsibly. Participants should not publish exploitable security flaws, private data, or harmful instructions. Findings should be reported through agreed professional channels.

modules.title: Vector Databases for Climate & Agritech
modules.asset: Highly specific local knowledge collections on soil conditions, rainfall changes, pest migration, crop cycles, fishing conditions, seed varieties, local climate adaptation, water access, and agricultural risk.
modules.execution: Participants collect field notes, extension-office documents, farmer interviews, market observations, crop calendars, soil reports, and local climate records. These materials are cleaned, tagged, and converted into a searchable knowledge base that can support Retrieval-Augmented Generation, or RAG.
modules.energy_maximisation: The first layer of work is offline: interviews, note-taking, document scanning, transcription, tagging, and file organisation. Embedding, uploading, and database syncing can happen later during power/connectivity windows.
modules.why_it_monetises: NGOs, agricultural companies, climate researchers, food-security programmes, and local cooperatives need accurate field-level data. Participants can sell data subscriptions, research briefs, map-linked reports, or RAG-ready knowledge packages.
modules.community_multiplier: Farmers, fishers, and cooperatives gain access to locally grounded information rather than generic climate or agriculture advice that may not match their land, water, or markets.
modules.safety_rule: Do not present AI-generated climate or agricultural recommendations as guaranteed expert advice. Verify information with local agricultural officers, experienced farmers, scientists, or trusted field organisations.

modules.title: Asynchronous Edge Crowdsourcing
modules.asset: Completed micro-task bundles such as object labeling, voice recording, map correction, translation feedback, image classification, local boundary mapping, and content review.
modules.execution: Participants use platforms such as Toloka, Clickworker, Appen, or similar task marketplaces. They download available task batches while connected, complete them offline or with minimal connectivity, and sync them later when they return to a charging station or connectivity hub.
modules.energy_maximisation: Participants work in airplane mode when possible, lower screen brightness, avoid video-heavy tasks unless paid well, and prioritise text, image-labeling, or short-audio tasks that can be completed in bursts.
modules.why_it_monetises: Micro-task platforms pay for human judgment that AI systems still need: labeling, correction, translation review, speech samples, location validation, and content moderation.
modules.community_multiplier: Participants learn the hidden labour behind AI systems and can later move from low-paid micro-tasks into higher-value annotation management, dataset design, or local AI quality assurance.
modules.safety_rule: Avoid exploitative platforms, unclear payment terms, disturbing content, or tasks that request private information. Participants should track time spent versus money earned to avoid digital underpayment.

modules.title: Decoupled Web-Scraping & Local Market Aggregation
modules.asset: Clean datasets on commodity prices, real estate availability, informal transit routes, school fees, clinic hours, fuel availability, water delivery, job postings, or local service prices.
modules.execution: Participants use mobile terminal tools, lightweight Python scripts, spreadsheets, or manual data collection to gather public information. They organise the results into CSV files, short reports, dashboards, or weekly market briefs.
modules.energy_maximisation: Text-only scripts and lightweight spreadsheets use less battery than visual browsing. Participants can run small collection scripts, save results locally, and upload only compressed CSV files when connected.
modules.why_it_monetises: Local logistics providers, small traders, researchers, NGOs, journalists, farmers, transport groups, and diaspora investors need fresh local data. Participants can sell weekly reports, subscriptions, or custom research.
modules.community_multiplier: Community members gain better information about prices, transport, housing, services, and opportunities. This reduces dependence on rumours or middlemen.
modules.safety_rule: Scrape only public, legally accessible data. Do not bypass passwords, paywalls, private groups, government restrictions, or platform rules. Verify scraped information with local human sources before selling it as reliable data.
